sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! Clicking on the purple ring pixel at (15, 56) moved the white diamond to the purple ring position! The white diamond is now at [[13, 56], [15, 58], [17, 56], [15, 54]], which is the purple ring position. This is similar to what happened in Level 1 when I clicked on purple ring pixels.
Now I need to continue clicking on purple ring pixels to complete the level. In Level 1, I clicked on multiple purple ring pixels in sequence. Let me continue clicking on the remaining purple ring pixels.
The purple ring has 12 pixels: (15, 56), (15, 58), (16, 55), (16, 59), (17, 54), (17, 60), (19, 54), (19, 60), (20, 55), (20, 59), (21, 56), (21, 58).
I already clicked on (15, 56). Let me continue clicking on the remaining pixels. But I need to figure out the order. In Level 1, I clicked on the purple ring pixels in a specific order (going around the ring).
Let me try clicking on the next purple ring pixel. Looking at the ring structure, the pixels form a diamond/ring shape. Let me click on (15, 58) next.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# White diamond moved to purple ring position! Now I need to click on all purple ring pixels.
# In Level 1, I clicked on purple ring pixels in sequence around the ring.
# Let me continue clicking on the remaining purple ring pixels.

# The purple ring pixels are:
# (15, 56) - already clicked
# (15, 58), (16, 55), (16, 59), (17, 54), (17, 60), (19, 54), (19, 60), (20, 55), (20, 59), (21, 56), (21, 58)

# Let me click on them in order around the ring
# Starting from top, going clockwise: (15, 58), (16, 59), (17, 60), (19, 60), (21, 58), (21, 56), (20, 55), (19, 54), (17, 54), (16, 55), (15, 56)
# But (15, 56) is already clicked, so I need to click on the remaining 11.

# Let me try clicking on all remaining purple ring pixels
purple_ring_remaining = [(15, 58), (16, 59), (17, 60), (19, 60), (21, 58), (21, 56), (20, 55), (19, 54), (17, 54), (16, 55)]

# Click on all of them
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in purple_ring_remaining]
action(actions)

print(f"After clicking purple ring pixels:")
print(f"Step: {current_frame.step}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over', 'N/A')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', 'N/A')}")

# Check white diamond position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] <= 20:
        print(f"White diamond: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
